summary: Matched the Yoshi Star item by replacing asm/melee/it/items/ityoshistar.s with src/melee/it/items/ityoshistar.c and adding a public ityoshistar.h. The C translation reconstructs the item spawn path, state table, animation/collision callbacks, owner and velocity initialization, gravity/fall-speed update, and rotation update. Build wiring was switched from the asm object to the C object, and related item prototypes/includes were cleaned up so the new item header owns the Yoshi Star declarations.

key_files.role: Deleted 314-line assembly source after matching it in C.
New matched C implementation for Yoshi Star item functions and state table it_803F7158.
New item-specific header exporting it_803F7158 and Yoshi Star function prototypes.
Changed TEXT_O_FILES from ityoshistar.s.o to ityoshistar.c.o.
Included ityoshistar.h and removed local extern/prototype declarations for Yoshi Star symbols.
Added shared item-system prototypes used by the new C file.
Removed now-redundant local item function prototypes, including itColl_BounceOffShield.

what_changed: Replaced the Yoshi Star assembly object with a C object in obj_files.mk.
Added ityoshistar.c implementing it_802B2FC8, it_802B309C, it_802B30C0, it_802B30E4, it_802B3108, it_802B312C, it_802B314C, it_802B322C, it_802B3294, it_802B32C8, it_802B3324, and it_802B3348.
Defined ItemStateTable it_803F7158[] in C with callbacks it_802B3294, it_802B32C8, and it_802B3324.
Introduced a local StarAttrs struct with speed and accel fields, read from item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes.
Reconstructed the spawn function using SpawnItem, It_Kind_Yoshi_Star, it_8026BB68, Item_80268B18, it_802B322C, and db_80225DD8.
Mapped four simple callbacks to it_80272BA4 followed by return true.
Mapped shield collision callback it_802B312C to return itColl_BounceOffShield(item_gobj).
Converted the rotation callback to call it_80273030 and HSD_JObjSetRotationY(jobj, M_PI / 2 * item->facing_dir), returning false.
Moved Yoshi Star declarations into a dedicated header and included it from it_803F3100.c.
Promoted shared helper declarations into it_266F.h and removed duplicate local declarations from itcapsule.c.
smart_moves: Kept the item-specific API in src/melee/it/items/ityoshistar.h instead of leaving anonymous externs in it_803F3100.c, which makes future item table work easier to include and audit.
Used a compact StarAttrs struct to document the two special-attribute fields proven by the assembly accesses: speed at offset 0 and accel at offset 4.
Used item macros and typed fields such as GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, item->owner, item->x40_vel, item->xCC_item_attr, and item->facing_dir to express the assembly behavior at the item-system level.
Recognized repeated callback bodies and represented them directly as small wrappers over it_80272BA4 returning true, reducing noise while preserving ABI-visible functions.
Used HSD_JObjSetRotationY rather than manually reproducing JObj flag checks, dirty-matrix updates, and assertions seen in the assembly, relying on the baselib helper to match the intended operation.
Changed the build object at the same time as deleting the assembly, avoiding a stale asm/C duplicate symbol situation.
decomp_lessons: When an item file is matched, add a dedicated item header and include it from item state-table aggregation files instead of accumulating local prototypes.
Small item-specific attribute blobs can often be named locally with only the fields used by the matched item; here speed and accel are enough evidence from x4_specialAttributes.
State table migration should move the table definition with the item source and remove the old extern from central files once the item header exports it.

Assembly patterns that write JObj rotation and dirty flags may correspond to HSD_JObjSetRotationY or related HSD_JObj setters; using the helper can avoid hand-coding internal JObj assertions and flag tests.

SpawnItem initialization order matters for matching: kind, previous position, computed position, facing direction, damage, zero velocity, parent pointers, spawn flag bit, and x40 are all explicitly reconstructed before Item_80268B18.
Repeated one-line item callbacks should still be kept as ABI-visible functions if the state table or external callers reference the original symbol addresses.
naming_conventions: New item files follow the existing lowercase item filename convention: ityoshistar.c and ityoshistar.h.
Unrenamed function symbols keep address-based names such as it_802B2FC8 and it_802B32C8.
The item state table remains address-named as it_803F7158.
The local special-attributes struct is named descriptively as StarAttrs with fields speed and accel.
Parameters use conventional item-system names such as item_gobj, parent, pos, dir, owner, and arg1.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The deleted assembly's sdata2 constants 0.0f and 0x3FC90FDB were represented as explicit 0.0F velocity initialization and M_PI / 2.
The assembly's stores to item velocity offsets were mapped to item->x40_vel.x and item->x40_vel.y.
The assembly's owner store at item offset 0x518 was represented as item->owner = owner.
The assembly's special-attribute reads through item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes were expressed as a typed StarAttrs pointer.

The assembly callback sequence it_80272860 followed by an x-velocity fused multiply-add became it_80272860(item_gobj, attr->x10_fall_speed, attr->x14_fall_speed_max) plus item->x40_vel.x += star->accel * item->facing_dir.
The assembly's null-check return pattern after Item_80268B18 was translated into an idiomatic if (item_gobj != NULL) return item_gobj; return NULL.
The JObj rotation path was matched through HSD_JObjSetRotationY, abstracting over the lower-level assembly writes, assertion strings, and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub call.

review_feedback.note: The PR context contains no issue comments, review comments, or review bodies, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.

follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename it_802B2FC8 or other Yoshi Star functions from address-based names.
Search for other item PRs using HSD_JObjSetRotationY to replace manual JObj rotation/dirty-flag assembly.
Find PRs that further type item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes for Yoshi special items.
Check whether it_266F.h later receives stronger declarations for it_80272860, it_8026B894, or related helpers still locally declared elsewhere.
Compare ityoshistar.c with neighboring matched item files such as ityoshieggthrow, itpikachutjoltground, and itsamusbomb for reusable spawn and state-table patterns.
